projects.raw_text:
Design of a Conventional Latch Type Sense Amplifier

Jan 2019 – Apr 2019


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Guide: Dr. Anuj Grover
Conventional Latch Type Sense Amplifier designed using 65nm technology node. Design started with the schematic to size the transistors appropriately such that delay from sense enable to the output latch is minimized. Then Eldo was used to evaluate the offset voltage of the SA. The layout of the design was generated, DRC and LVS were performed. Finally, post-layout extractions were performed.

Skills: Problem Solving, Transistors

Verilog Game Design on FPGA

Jan 2019 – Apr 2019


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Guide: Dr. Sumit Darak
The Snow Hunter game on 7-series FPGA using Verilog HDL was designed. VGA module controls the monitor screen. The theme of this game is “eat smaller snowman, avoid bigger snowman’’. Eating snowmen increase your size to eat bigger snowman and finally to eat giant. Carrot, nitro, and clock will give special powers. On the other hand obstacles like ice cubes and trees should be avoided.

Skills: Problem Solving

Digital VLSI Design

Aug 2018 – Nov 2018


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Guide: Dr. Anuj Grover
Complex Gate AOI32 was designed using 65 nm technology node. Design started with the schematic to size the transistors appropriately and to decide the number of stages based on certain conditions. The layout of the design was generated after that DRC and LVS were performed. In addition to the typical corner analysis, the design was analyzed at the SS corner for the worst-case performance and at the FF corner for the worst-case power consumption.

Skills: Problem Solving, Transistors

Sub-threshold CMOS Op-Amp Design

Aug 2018 – Nov 2018


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Guide: Dr. G.S. Visweswaran
Subthreshold design of low voltage low power two stages Miller capacitance Operational Amplifier was designed using 180nm technology node. Started with the basic electronics and equations in the subthreshold region, low voltage low power operational amplifier had been designed that achieved the expected specifications. Design demonstrated a very efficient solution to the modern electronic system such as portable biomedical applications or in the analog section of mixed-signal integrated circuits.

Skills: Problem Solving, Transistors

VLSI Design Flow

Aug 2018 – Nov 2018


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Guide: Dr. Sneh Saurabh
RTL code was developed in Verilog and synthesized using RTL Compiler. Different netlist with constraints like area, power, and performance was generated. Equivalence checking for the generated netlist was done using Conformal. Static Timing Analysis was done and results like time slack for the design were observed using Tempus. The Encounter was used to generate the Physical Design for the netlist. 

Skills: Problem Solving, Transistors
